input.title: Hotel Booking Platform
input.description: A comprehensive online hotel booking platform designed to connect travelers with accommodations. Users can search for hotels, view detailed property pages, check real-time room availability, and make secure reservations. Hotel managers have access to a dedicated dashboard to manage their properties, update room inventory, set dynamic pricing, and oversee bookings. An admin panel allows platform owners to moderate listings and manage users.

user_classes.Guest: Unauthenticated visitor who can search for hotels, view availability, and browse reviews but must register to make a booking.
user_classes.Customer: Registered user who can book rooms, manage upcoming and past reservations, save favorite hotels, and leave post-stay reviews.
user_classes.Hotel Manager: Property owner or staff who manages hotel profiles, room types, pricing, availability calendars, and views customer bookings.
user_classes.Admin: Platform administrator who oversees the entire system, approves new hotel listings, moderates reviews, and manages user roles.

definitions.Property: A hotel, resort, or accommodation listed on the platform.
definitions.Room Type: A specific category of room within a property (e.g., Deluxe Double, Standard Single).
definitions.Inventory: The number of available rooms for a specific Room Type on a given date.
definitions.OTA: Online Travel Agency — the category of platform this system belongs to.

scope: User registration for customers and hotel managers
Advanced hotel search by destination, dates, guests, and filters
Secure checkout with Stripe integration for deposits and full payments
Property management dashboard for hotels to manage inventory
Email notifications for booking confirmations and reminders
out_of_scope: Integrated loyalty point system in the initial release
Channel manager integration with external OTAs (e.g., Booking.com, Expedia)
constraints: The platform must use PostgreSQL for relational data and Redis for caching search queries
Payment processing must be entirely handled by Stripe to maintain PCI compliance
Images must be optimized and stored in a cloud CDN (e.g., AWS S3 or Cloudinary)
Search results must load in under 1 second to ensure a competitive user experience

operating_environment.frontend: React/Next.js application, responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports, deployed on Vercel.
operating_environment.backend: Node.js Express API RESTful services deployed on AWS or Heroku.
operating_environment.database: PostgreSQL managed database, with Redis for session and search caching.

FR-01.title: User Authentication and Access Control
requirements: Users can register as a Customer or request a Hotel Manager account
Login must support email/password and social login (Google)
Hotel Manager accounts require Admin approval before they can list properties
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to separate Customer, Manager, and Admin routes
Password reset functionality via email link

FR-02.title: Hotel Search and Filtering
requirements: Homepage search bar requires destination, check-in, check-out dates, and number of guests/rooms
Search results must return properties with at least one available room matching the criteria
Results can be filtered by price range, star rating, amenities (e.g., pool, wifi), and property type
Users can sort results by recommended, price (low to high), price (high to low), and distance from city center
Pagination or infinite scroll for search results

FR-03.title: Property and Room Details
requirements: Each property page displays a photo gallery, description, amenities, location map, and aggregate review score
Room availability table shows available room types for the selected dates, with photos, details, and price per night
Dynamic pricing is calculated based on the selected date range
Users can select the number of rooms of a specific type to book

FR-04.title: Booking Engine and Checkout
requirements: Checkout process requires guest details, special requests, and estimated arrival time
System must place a temporary hold (lock) on the room inventory for 15 minutes during checkout
Price summary breaks down room rate, taxes, and platform service fees
Integration with Stripe Payment Intents to securely process credit cards
Upon successful payment, an itinerary number is generated and inventory is permanently updated

FR-05.title: Customer Dashboard
requirements: Customers can view active, upcoming, and past reservations
Users can download invoice receipts for their bookings
Cancellation feature allows users to cancel bookings subject to the property's cancellation policy
Users can manage their profile, saved payment methods, and favorite properties

FR-06.title: Hotel Manager Portal
requirements: Managers can create and edit property profiles (name, address, policies, photos)
Managers can define room types and set base prices
Inventory calendar tool allows managers to block out dates, adjust daily availability, and override pricing for peak seasons
Dashboard shows a list of upcoming bookings, occupancy rates, and revenue metrics
Managers can view customer details for upcoming reservations

FR-07.title: Reviews and Ratings
requirements: Only customers with a verified completed stay can leave a review for a property
Reviews involve a 1-5 star rating across categories (cleanliness, location, service, value) and a text comment
Property overall rating is a weighted average of individual category scores
Hotel Managers can post a public reply to user reviews
Users can report inappropriate reviews to the Admin

FR-08.title: Admin Moderation Panel
requirements: Admins can review and approve pending Hotel Manager account requests
Admins can view platform-wide metrics (total bookings, active users, revenue)
Admins can moderate properties (suspend or remove listings that violate terms)
Admins can resolve reported reviews and manage platform-wide configurations

NFR-01.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Search queries involving date and location must return within 800ms
The system must handle concurrent bookings without double-booking a single room
Images must be lazy-loaded and served in next-gen formats (WebP)

NFR-02.title: Security and Privacy
requirements: Passwords must securely hashed using Argon2 or bcrypt
PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in databases must be encrypted at rest
All API traffic must be secured via TLS 1.3

system_attributes.reliability: Inventory locking mechanism ensures 99.9% prevention of overbooking during high-concurrency periods.
system_attributes.security: PCI-DSS compliance achieved by outsourcing payment data capture directly to Stripe.
system_attributes.availability: Critical search APIs are cached in Redis to maintain high availability during traffic spikes.

FR-01.title: Design Authentication and Onboarding UI
FR-01.description: Create high-fidelity mockups for customer and hotel manager registration, login screens, and password recovery. Include multi-step onboarding for managers to request access.
acceptance_criteria: Clean, responsive layouts for login and signup on mobile and desktop.
Clear visual distinction between Customer and Partner (Manager) registration.
Design accommodates Google single sign-on buttons.

FR-01.title: Implement User Registration and Authentication API
FR-01.description: Build Node.js endpoints for user signup and login. Implement password hashing using bcrypt. Set up JWT-based session management and role assignment in the database payload.
acceptance_criteria: Signup endpoint validates email format and password strength.
Login endpoint returns a valid JWT with the user's role and ID.
Manager signups are flagged with 'status: pending_approval' by default.

FR-01.title: Develop Frontend Authentication Flows
FR-01.description: Implement React forms for registration and login. Integrate with the backend API to handle JWT storage. Establish protected routes based on user role (Customer vs. Manager vs. Admin).
acceptance_criteria: Forms handle inline validation and present server errors gracefully.
Successful login redirects the user to the correct dashboard based on role.
Unauthenticated users trying to access protected routes are redirected to login.

FR-01.title: Setup OAuth via Google
FR-01.description: Configure Google Cloud Console OAuth credentials and implement the OAuth 2.0 flow securely on the backend, generating a JWT for the frontend upon successful social login.
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in with Google account seamlessly.
If the user email doesn't exist, a new Customer account is created automatically.

FR-02.title: Design Search Interface and Results Page
FR-02.description: Wireframe and design the homepage search widget (Destination, Dates, Guests) and the search results page showing hotel cards, a map view toggle, and comprehensive filter sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: Search bar is highly visible and intuitive, with a custom date picker design.
Results page includes a responsive filter drawer for mobile users.
Hotel cards highlight price, rating, and a key thumbnail image.

FR-02.title: Implement Available Inventory Search Service
FR-02.description: Develop the core search POST endpoint. The query must join the Property table with the Room Inventory table, filtering out properties that do not have enough available rooms for the requested dates.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint accepts location (city/text), checkIn, checkOut, and guest count.
Returns only active properties that have real-time availability.
Optimized SQL query ensures response times remain under 800ms.

FR-02.title: Build Search Components and Date Picker
FR-02.description: Implement the frontend search bar widget using a reliable date-range picker library. Ensure URL parameters are updated via React Router when a search is triggered to allow shareable search links.
acceptance_criteria: Date picker prevents selecting past dates or check-out dates before check-in.
Search parameters serialize into the URL query string.
Map view integrates Google Maps or Mapbox API to display hotel pins.

FR-02.title: Create Search Indexes and View Optimization
FR-02.description: Analyze the search queries and create necessary B-tree or GiST indexes on the database (e.g., location strings, pricing, review stats) to ensure scaling under heavy read load.
acceptance_criteria: EXPLAIN ANALYZE confirms index usage on the primary search queries.
Location text search utilizes optimized Postgres text-search functionality.

FR-03.title: Design Property Detail Page UI
FR-03.description: Create the layout for the property detail view. Must feature a prominent media gallery, a clean list of amenities, policies, an interactive room selection table for pricing, and a reviews section.
acceptance_criteria: Media gallery supports a hero image with a grid of thumbnails.
Room selection table displays clear pricing per night based on user date context.

FR-03.title: Build Property Profile API
FR-03.description: Develop GET endpoints to retrieve comprehensive property details by ID/Slug, including nested room types, available amenities, active policies, and recent reviews.
acceptance_criteria: API aggregates data from Properties, Rooms, Amenities, and Reviews tables.
Returns pre-calculated average ratings.

FR-03.title: Develop Property Detail and Room Selector Frontend
FR-03.description: Implement the React page for property details. Create the interactive room table where users can select multiple rooms and see a real-time summary of the total price before advancing to checkout.
acceptance_criteria: Room table accurately reflects availability for the selected dates.
'Reserve' button is disabled if no rooms are selected.
Image gallery supports lightbox mode for full-screen viewing.

FR-04.title: Implement Inventory Locking Mechanism
FR-04.description: Build an API endpoint for initiating checkout that uses Redis to place a temporary 15-minute lock on the selected room inventory. If checkout expires, the lock is released.
acceptance_criteria: Two users attempting to book the last room simultaneously will result in only one lock succeeding.
Lock automatically expires and inventory is freed after 15 minutes without payment.

FR-04.title: Integrate Stripe Payment Gateway
FR-04.description: Implement Stripe Payment Intents API on the backend. Create the payment processing webhook to confirm successful charges, finalize bookings, and deduct inventory permanently.
acceptance_criteria: Payment amount strictly matches the server-calculated total, not client-supplied values.
Webhook safely handles idempotency to avoid duplicate order creation.
Successful payment updates database Booking status to 'Confirmed'.

FR-04.title: Build Checkout Flow and Forms UI
FR-04.description: Develop the multi-step checkout frontend. Include guest detail collection, a 15-minute countdown timer for the reservation hold, and the Stripe Elements card input component.
acceptance_criteria: Countdown timer clearly indicates when the reservation hold will expire.
Stripe Elements component handles credit card validation inline.
Upon success, user is routed to a structured Booking Confirmation page.

FR-04.title: Write End-to-End Booking Tests
FR-04.description: Create automated tests for the entire booking flow: search -> select room -> acquire lock -> mock stripe payment -> confirm booking.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent booking attempts for limited inventory are handled gracefully.
Booking creation precisely updates available inventory logic.
Failed payment resets the lock appropriately.

FR-05.title: Build Customer Dashboard API
FR-05.description: Create REST endpoints for customers to retrieve their reservations, profile data, and saved properties. Implement cancellation logic that respects the hotel's timestamped cancellation policy.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/user/bookings returns paginated reservations.
POST /api/user/bookings/:id/cancel calculates refunds based on policy and triggers Stripe refund if applicable.

FR-05.title: Develop Customer Portal UI
FR-05.description: Implement the frontend interface for the user dashboard. Group bookings into 'Upcoming', 'Past', and 'Cancelled' tabs. Provide UI for triggering a cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard is accessible only to authenticated Customers.
Cancellation button warns user of any fees before confirming action.

FR-06.title: Design Hotel Manager Portal
FR-06.description: Create the UX/UI for the Hotel Manager backend. Focus on the inventory management calendar and the booking overview dashboard, making it intuitive to adjust availability and prices.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar UI cleanly handles multiple room types and daily price inputs.
Dashboard highlights critical action items like new bookings and low occupancy.

FR-06.title: Implement Property Management CRUD API
FR-06.description: Build secured backend endpoints allowing managers to create and edit properties, upload photos via S3, define room types, and establish default amenities.
acceptance_criteria: Managers can only modify properties linked to their account ID.
Cloudinary/S3 integration handles image uploads and optimization.

FR-06.title: Develop Pricing and Inventory Calendar UI
FR-06.description: Build an advanced React calendar component for managers. Managers must be able to select date ranges, block out specific rooms (e.g., for maintenance), and adjust daily rates dynamically.
acceptance_criteria: Bulk editing feature applies changes to a range of dates efficiently.
Visual indicators show days where rooms are fully booked.

FR-07.title: Create Review Submission API
FR-07.description: Implement an endpoint allowing users to post reviews. Validate that the user has a completed booking for the target property to prevent spam.
acceptance_criteria: API enforces one review per completed reservation.
Triggers async job to recalculate the property's average aggregate rating.

FR-07.title: Build Review Frontend and Manager Responses
FR-07.description: Create the review submission form. Also, add functionality in the Manager dashboard allowing managers to submit a public response to individual reviews.
acceptance_criteria: Review modal allows star inputs across 4 categories.
Manager responses are displayed clearly beneath the corresponding user review.

FR-08.title: Develop Super-Admin Moderation Tools
FR-08.description: Build the admin interface to manage system activity. Include views to approve/deny pending Hotel Manager accounts and suspend problematic property listings.
acceptance_criteria: Admin dashboard restricted strongly by RBAC middleware.
Approving a Manager automatically triggers a welcome/activation email.
